target.title: feat(browser): extend --labels overlay to full-page and element captures
target.body: Bring extensions/browser '--labels' screenshot to its full intended scope: full-page captures, element/ref captures, and a per-ref bounding-box metadata array. - '--labels --full-page' now actually projects labels across the entire page (previously silently dropped fullPage in the Playwright path). - '--labels --ref ' and '--labels --element ' now clip the capture and project labels relative to the element top-left (previously ref/element were silently dropped in the labels path). - Response includes a new 'annotations' array of per-ref bounding boxes ({ref, number, role, name?, box: {x,y,width,height}}) so agents can reason about positions without re-snapshotting. The field is omitted when empty. Implementation: - New screenshot-annotate.ts pure helper: refToNumber, planAnnotations with viewport/fullpage/element coordinate projection (with overlap filter for element mode), buildOverlayInjectionScript and buildOverlayClearScript IIFE generators. Zero Playwright dependency so the helper is reusable for the raw-CDP path in a follow-up. - screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright rewritten to consume the helper, with off-viewport refs no longer filtered (drawn in document space and clipped by the screenshot capture instead). - /screenshot Playwright branch forwards fullPage/ref/element through to the helper and returns annotations. - /snapshot Playwright branch returns annotations only (snapshot remains intentionally viewport-only and does not gain new params). - LLM-facing safeDetails surfaces annotations to the agent. Behavior changes (documented in docs/cli/browser.md and SKILL.md): - labelsSkipped no longer counts off-viewport refs (only maxLabels truncation and per-ref bbox failures count). - Existing fullPage + (ref|element) 400 validation is unchanged. chrome-mcp and raw-CDP paths are deliberately unchanged in this change. existing-session profiles continue to omit the annotations field. That is documented as a known downgrade and tracked for follow-up. Tests: - screenshot-annotate.test.ts: 24 cases covering refToNumber forms, three coordinate-space projections, maxLabels truncation, empty-name omission, and IIFE script content/idempotency. - pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts: 7 mock-based cases covering all three capture modes, finally-cleanup-on-error, off-viewport-not-filtered, and bboxFailures accounting. - All existing 1192 extensions/browser tests still pass. ## Summary What problem does this PR solve? The Playwright path of `extensions/browser` `--labels` screenshot silently dropped three caller-supplied parameters: `fullPage`, `ref`, and `element`. A user passing `--labels --full-page` got a viewport-only capture with no error. A user passing `--labels --ref e3` got a viewport capture of the whole page, ignoring the element scoping. The labels response also gave the LLM no way to reason about a ref's on-image position without re-running snapshot, so multimodal agents had to re-snapshot for every layout question. Why does this matter now? The `--labels` flag is the workhorse for vision-capable agents working on long pages or single components. The silent-drop made any prompt-engineered combination such as "screenshot the form with labels" produce wrong-but-not-erroring output, which is a worse failure mode than a clear 4xx. Adding the `annotations` array is the single biggest information-density gain for that prompt path: positions become a few hundred bytes of structured JSON instead of a re-issued snapshot RPC. What is the intended outcome? - `--labels --full-page` produces a full-page PNG/JPG with labels in document coordinates, all the way to the bottom of the page. - `--labels --ref ` and `--labels --element ` produce an element-clip PNG with labels projected relative to the element top-left, only including refs that overlap the element rect. - Every Playwright-path labels response includes `annotations: [{ref, number, role, name?, box}]` (omitted when empty). - A pure helper (`screenshot-annotate.ts`) holds all coordinate math and DOM-injection IIFE strings, so the same logic can be reused by the raw-CDP path later without refactor. What is intentionally out of scope? - chrome-mcp path. `existing-session` profiles continue to use `renderChromeMcpLabels` and continue to omit the `annotations` field; this is a documented downgrade. - Raw-CDP fallback. Routes that pre-empt to `captureScreenshot()` still do not produce labels (was already the case). - The `snapshot` route does not gain `fullPage / ref / element` parameters; the accompanying screenshot stays viewport-only. - No new CLI flag. `--labels` keeps its current shape; only the help text and the response payload were updated. - No `CHANGELOG.md` edits (per CONTRIBUTING.md). What does success look like? A long page (`https://playwright.dev/`, 4121px tall) labeled with `--labels --full-page` returns a JPEG that has annotation boxes drawn at `y` values up to 4121, an `annotations` array of 64 entries with `box.y` matching the same range, and `labelsSkipped: 0`. After the capture, `document.querySelectorAll('[data-openclaw-labels]').length === 0` (no DOM residue). What should reviewers focus on? - Coordinate-space correctness: `planAnnotations` projects `box` relative to viewport scroll for `viewport`, returns doc-space for `fullpage`, and projects relative to `elementRect` (with an overlap filter) for `element`. Tests in `screenshot-annotate.test.ts` lock each axis. - Cleanup invariant: `buildOverlayClearScript()` MUST run even when `page.screenshot()` throws. Covered by `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` with a screenshot mock that throws. - The semantic narrowing of `labelsSkipped` (no longer counts off-viewport refs). Documented in the Behavior changes section above and in docs/cli/browser.md. - Whether the `/snapshot` Playwright branch should be left out of fullPage/ref/element pass-through. I argue yes; the snapshot command is intentionally a "snapshot text + viewport screenshot" surface. ## Linked context Which issue does this close? Closes # (No tracking issue exists. The behavior gaps were discovered while building a multimodal browser agent prompt and the `--labels` family is the natural seam for the fix.) Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related? Related # (None I am aware of. If a maintainer points to one I will link it.) Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No. Drive-by contribution. I am happy to close the PR if a maintainer wants the work to land via a different seam (e.g. a separate `--annotate` flag, or held until raw-CDP/chrome-mcp parity is in scope). The change is self-contained: deleting the new helper file and reverting `screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright` removes the feature cleanly. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: - `--labels --full-page` was producing viewport-only output (silently dropping `fullPage`). - `--labels --ref ` was producing whole-page viewport output (silently dropping `ref`). - `--labels` responses had no per-ref bounding box data; agents had to re-snapshot to learn positions. - Real environment tested: - OS: Linux 6.8.0-101-generic x86_64 - Node: v24.16.0 - Browser: headless Chromium 1223 (Playwright-bundled), driver `openclaw`, `cdpPort 9222` - Profile: `--profile dev` running `pnpm dev --profile dev gateway run` (source mode, freshly-built code paths) - Target page: `https://playwright.dev/` (4121px tall scrollable document) - Head: `3731eb15eb3b545b6211b27f0b28f36905bd938b` (the current HEAD; all evidence below was re-captured against this SHA after the latest force-push) - Exact steps or command run after this patch: ``` alias oc='node openclaw.mjs --profile dev' oc browser start oc browser navigate https://playwright.dev/ oc browser focus # Case 1: snapshot with annotations oc browser --json snapshot --interactive --labels # Case 2: viewport screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot --labels # Case 3: full-page screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot --labels --full-page # Case 4: element-clip screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot --labels --ref e3 # Case 5: DOM cleanup check oc browser evaluate --fn "() => document.querySelectorAll('[data-openclaw-labels]').length" ``` - Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Three live screenshots from `https://playwright.dev/`: **Viewport mode** (`screenshot --labels`) — labels visible only in the captured viewport region: **Full-page mode** (`screenshot --labels --full-page`) — 4121px-tall capture; labels span the entire scrollable document, including content far below the fold: **Element-clip mode** (`screenshot --labels --ref e3`) — capture is clipped to the element rect; labels are projected relative to the element top-left: Sample response (full-page mode at HEAD `3731eb15eb`, copied live; saved image is `401x2000` so coordinates are auto-rescaled by `scaleAnnotations()`): ```json { "ok": true, "path": "/path/to/screenshot.jpg", "url": "https://playwright.dev/", "labels": true, "labelsCount": 64, "labelsSkipped": 0, "annotations": [ { "ref": "e3", "number": 3, "role": "navigation", "name": "Main", "box": { "x": 0, "y": 0, "width": 358, "height": 28 } } ] } ``` Sample response (element-clip with `--ref e3`), copied live: ```json { "labelsCount": 4, "annotations": [ { "ref": "e3", "number": 3, "role": "navigation", "name": "Main", "box": { "x": 0, "y": 0, "width": 765, "height": 60 } }, { "ref": "e4", "number": 4, "role": "button", "name": "Toggle navigation bar", "box": { "x": 16, "y": 15, "width": 30, "height": 30 } } ] } ``` - Observed result after fix: | # | Verification | Observed at HEAD `3731eb15eb` | |---|---|---| | 1 | snapshot --labels --json | 64 annotations with `{ref, number, role, name, box}`; numbers correctly parsed from refs (`e3 → 3`) | | 2 | screenshot --labels --json (viewport) | Saved PNG `780×437`; 64 annotations; `labelsSkipped: 0` confirms semantic narrowing. By design, off-viewport refs keep their viewport-relative `box.y` (can exceed image height) so the agent can request a scroll/full-page re-capture instead of seeing them silently dropped. | | 3 | screenshot --labels --full-page --json | Saved JPEG `401×2000` (resized from original `780×4121` by `normalizeBrowserScreenshot`); 64 annotations rescaled accordingly; `box.y` range `0..1928`, all within image height; **`max(box.y)=1928 ≤ image.height=2000` confirms the rescale fix** (was `0..4121` before the rescale was added). | | 4 | screenshot --labels --ref e3 --json | Saved element-clip PNG `765×60`; 4 annotations all in element-relative coordinates (element itself at `x=0, y=0`); `width=765` and `height=60` match the saved image. | | 5 | DOM cleanup query | `document.querySelectorAll('[data-openclaw-labels]').length` returns `0`; no overlay residue. | - What was not tested: - chrome-mcp path on `existing-session` profile. Code review confirms it is bypassed (the labels branch checks `usesChromeMcpProfile(profile.driver)` first and routes to `renderChromeMcpLabels`). The `annotations` field is intentionally absent in that branch's response. - Raw-CDP fallback (`captureScreenshot()` when Playwright is unavailable). Code review confirms the `--labels` path routes through Playwright; the fallback is unchanged. - macOS / Windows. Linux only. The change does not touch any OS-specific code paths. - Real `existing-session` regression. I do not have a Chrome 144+ remote debugging session set up locally; the touched route file leaves the chrome-mcp branch byte-identical at the point of the labels call, so I do not believe regression is possible there. - Proof limitations or environment constraints: - Headless mode only. I do not have a desktop environment in this VM. Headed-mode behavior should be identical because the screenshot path is the same `page.screenshot()` / `locator.screenshot()` call, but I did not verify visually in headed mode. - The dev profile uses an internal-only model provider for the LLM-facing case (verified by reading `safeDetails` propagation in code, not by full agent loop). - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this fix on the same page and same gateway, `screenshot --labels --full-page` produced a 768x720 PNG with labels visible only in the top viewport; the rest of the document (below the fold) had no labels and no boxes. `screenshot --labels --ref e3` produced an identical viewport PNG, ignoring the ref. The response did not contain an `annotations` field. Reproducible by checking out the parent commit (`aa75477533`) and re-running steps 3 and 4 above. - Live terminal capture (HEAD `3731eb15eb`): This was captured immediately before the PR body was updated, by re-running the L2 evidence harness against the current HEAD. The key invariant is `max(box.y) <= image.height`, which is the exact contract ClawSweeper asked for ("annotation coordinates match the saved image after normalization"). ``` L2 EVIDENCE — HEAD = 3731eb15eb3b545b6211b27f0b28f36905bd938b === [Case 1] viewport: screenshot --labels === saved image: 780 x 437 (PNG) labelsCount: 64, labelsSkipped: 0 annotations: 64 entries, box.y range: 0..4121.0625 note: viewport mode keeps off-viewport refs at viewport coords by design === [Case 2] full-page: screenshot --labels --full-page === saved image: 401 x 2000 (JPEG; resized from original 780 x 4121 by normalizeBrowserScreenshot) labelsCount: 64, labelsSkipped: 0 annotations: 64 entries, box.y range: 0..1928 check: max(box.y)=1928 <= image.height=2000: True sample annotation: {'ref': 'e3', 'number': 3, 'role': 'navigation', 'name': 'Main', 'box': {'x': 0, 'y': 0, 'width': 358, 'height': 28}} === [Case 3] element-clip: screenshot --labels --ref e3 === saved image: 765 x 60 (PNG) labelsCount: 4, labelsSkipped: 0 annotations: 4 entries, all in element-relative coords: ref=e3 role=navigation box={x: 0, y: 0, width: 765, height: 60} ref=e4 role=button box={x: 16, y: 15, width: 30, height: 30} ref=e5 role=link box={x: 54, y: 14, width: 136.59375, height: 32} ref=e6 role=button box={x: 593.640625, y: 12, width: 155.359375, height: 36} === [Case 4] DOM cleanup === document.querySelectorAll('[data-openclaw-labels]').length → 0 ``` ## Tests and validation Which commands did you run? ``` pnpm tsgo:extensions pnpm test extensions/browser pnpm check:changed ``` All green. `pnpm test extensions/browser` reports `1285 passed | 1 skipped (1286)` at HEAD `3731eb15eb`. What regression coverage was added or updated? - New `extensions/browser/src/browser/screenshot-annotate.test.ts` (24 cases). Pure helper unit tests with no mocks. Covers `refToNumber` for `e`, `ax`, bare numeric, and non-numeric refs; viewport / fullpage / element coordinate projection; element-mode overlap filter; `maxLabels` truncation with `skipped` accounting; empty-name omission; IIFE injection-script content (data-openclaw-labels attr, root id, item coordinates); IIFE clear-script idempotency; empty-input safety. - New `extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` (7 cases). Mock-based via `pw-tools-core.test-harness.ts`. Covers viewport mode (no fullPage forwarded; annotation projection correct), fullpage mode (`fullPage: true` forwarded; doc-space `box.y` retained), element/ref mode (`refLocator(...).screenshot()` called; annotations projected relative to element rect), `finally`-cleanup-on-error (clear script runs even when screenshot throws), off-viewport refs not filtered, and `bboxFailures` accounting (refs whose `boundingBox()` returns null contribute to `skipped`). What failed before this fix, if known? No automated test failed on `main`. The behavior gaps were silent-drop bugs: parameters were accepted by the route schema and passed through several layers before being dropped at the inner Playwright function call site without any check. CI did not flag them because there was no test asserting that `--full-page --labels` actually produced a full-page output, and no test asserting `--ref --labels` actually produced an element clip. If no test was added, why not? N/A. Tests added for every new code path. I did not add a route-level integration test covering Playwright-path labels on a non-existing-session profile. The existing route test file `agent.snapshot.local-managed.test.ts` is structured around `requirePwAi: vi.fn(async () => null)`, intentionally exercising the no-Playwright fallback. Adding a parallel Playwright-path harness for marginal coverage felt low-ROI given that the helper layer (24 cases) plus the integration layer (7 cases) plus the live behavior proof above already lock the relevant paths. Happy to add it if a maintainer prefers. ## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes. Three documented behavior changes: - `--labels --full-page` now produces full-page output. Previously produced viewport-only output silently. Any caller that relied on the silent drop would now see additional labels; I do not believe any caller relies on silently-broken output. - `--labels --ref e3` and `--labels --element ` now produce element-clip output. Previously produced viewport-only output silently. Same compatibility argument. - `labelsSkipped` no longer counts off-viewport refs. The number can only decrease for the same input. Callers that read `labelsSkipped` to detect "too many refs" still work; callers that treat it as "refs not visible in the screenshot" need to switch to filtering `annotations` by image bounds, but I am not aware of such a caller. Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No. No config schema changes, no migrations, no new env vars, no plugin SDK surface changes. Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No. No new permissions. No changes to SSRF policy enforcement (the existing `assertBrowserNavigationResultAllowed` and `withBrowserNavigationPolicy` calls are unchanged). No new network destinations. No changes to the secret-handling surface. No tool execution changes; `evaluate` and `act` paths are untouched. What is the highest-risk area? Coordinate-space invariants in `planAnnotations`. The `box` field MUST be in the captured image's coordinate space (viewport-relative for viewport mode, document-relative for fullpage mode, element-relative for element mode). A bug here means agents click on the wrong screen pixel. How is that risk mitigated? - Three coordinate-space projection tests in `screenshot-annotate.test.ts` lock the math. - `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` asserts `fullPage: true` is forwarded and `box.y` is in doc-space (combined assertion at the integration boundary). - Live proof on a 4121px page shows `box.y` values reaching 4121 in fullpage mode but capped at viewport in viewport mode, behaving correctly under both modes. - Element-clip live proof shows the captured element appears at `box: { x: 0, y: 0 }`, confirming the element-relative projection. Secondary risk: DOM residue if cleanup fails. Mitigated by the unconditional `finally { ... clearScript ... }` block in `screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright`, the dedicated cleanup-on-error test, and the live `[data-openclaw-labels].length === 0` proof. ## Current review state What is the next action? Maintainer review. What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Nothing on author. Awaiting CI to settle and a maintainer's call on: - Whether the chrome-mcp / raw-CDP downgrade is acceptable as a follow-up, vs. blocking on parity here. - Whether you would prefer a route-level Playwright-path integration test added before merge. Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? None yet (initial PR).

target.title: fix(ui): add agent selector to dreaming tab
target.body:
## Summary

- Closes #63558.
- The Control UI Dreaming tab needs to work across agent contexts. Without a selector and scoped reloads, users could switch visible context but still see the default agent's dreaming status, diary, or related memory state.
- This PR adds Dreaming agent selection, scopes Dreaming status/diary reloads to the selected agent, and keeps backward-compatible default-agent fallback when no agent id is supplied.
- It also opens report palace cards as wiki previews from Dreaming so report links stay in the Control UI review flow.
- Out of scope: changing Dreaming storage semantics, changing auth/network/tool execution behavior, or broad Control UI routing refactors.
- Review focus: selected-agent propagation through the Dreaming UI/controller/gateway path, report-card preview routing, and the generated i18n updates once the separate merge-conflict item is resolved.

## Linked context

Closes #63558.

Related context:
- This PR is a targeted Control UI/Dreaming fix for agent-specific Dreaming context.
- The current branch also has a separate tracked upstream merge conflict in generated Control UI i18n metadata/translation files; that conflict is not part of this body-only update.

Maintainer/owner request:
- No separate maintainer request beyond the linked issue and PR review automation.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Dreaming lacked reliable selected-agent behavior. Agent selection in the Dreaming tab needed to scope `dreamingStatus` and `dreamDiary` loads to the selected agent instead of falling back to the default context.
- Real environment tested: local `openclaw/openclaw` checkout on Linux, Node.js v24.14.0, vitest v4.1.5.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts`
  - Earlier UI proof on this PR: `cd ui && pnpm vitest run src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - Earlier support checks on this PR: `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm lint:core`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

~~~text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.5 /tmp/pincer-openclaw

 ✓  gateway-methods  src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts (27 tests) 89ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  27 passed (27)
    Start at  00:25:12
    Duration  470ms
~~~

~~~text
✓ src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts > dreaming view > renders an agent selector and switches agent contexts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       16 passed (16)
~~~

- Observed result after fix: `onSelectAgent` records the selected agent id, Dreaming reloads status and diary data scoped to that agent, gateway handlers accept optional `agentId`, and omitted `agentId` continues to resolve through the default-agent fallback.
- What was not tested: live browser interaction against a running multi-agent gateway.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this update only repairs the PR body template. The branch still has a separate generated i18n merge-tree conflict against current `upstream/main`, so this run did not push code or rerun the full branch validation suite.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before the fix, Dreaming selection could call the session switch path while the subsequent `doctor.memory.*` loads still used `{}`, leaving the tab on default-agent Dreaming data.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

For this body repair:
- Fetched the current upstream `.github/pull_request_template.md` for the PR base branch through the authenticated GitHub API.
- Updated the PR body through the authenticated GitHub API.
- Re-read the live PR body through the authenticated GitHub API and verified every current top-level template heading is present.

Existing code validation recorded for this PR:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts`
- `cd ui && pnpm vitest run src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm lint:core`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `ui/src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts` covers rendering the agent selector and changing agent context.
- `ui/src/ui/controllers/dreaming.test.ts` covers Dreaming controller selected-agent behavior.
- `src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts` covers optional agent-id handling and default fallback for the gateway methods.
- `ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts` covers active-tab refresh state touched by the Dreaming flow.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Dreaming did not expose the same agent-selection control expected in multi-agent Control UI contexts.
- Selected-agent changes could leave Dreaming status/diary loads scoped to the default agent rather than the chosen agent.

If no test was added, why not?

- N/A. The branch includes focused UI/controller/gateway regression coverage. This body-only worker item did not add code.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Showing Dreaming data for the wrong agent, or routing report/wiki preview interactions through the wrong Control UI context.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The selector and scoped reload paths are covered by focused Dreaming UI/controller tests and gateway method tests.
- Omitted `agentId` remains backward compatible through the existing default-agent fallback.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- PR body template compliance is addressed by this update.
- The remaining branch action is the already-tracked generated Control UI i18n conflict/current-head check failure, not this body-only item.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Author/branch work remains blocked separately on generated `ui/src/i18n/.i18n/*` merge-tree conflicts against current `upstream/main`.
- The latest tracked current-head `checks-node-agentic-agents` failure remains associated with that branch-conflict/validation path.
- Prior worker notes record that local conflict resolution hit gateway-watch validation outside the body-update scope.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- The current-template PR body gap is addressed here by preserving every current upstream template heading with concrete content.

target.title: Agents/exec: show target node name in exec tool transparency messages
target.body:
## Summary

When the `exec` tool runs on a remote node (`host=node, node=<name>`), the tool-call transparency message currently shows only the command — there's no indication of which node the command ran on. This makes it hard to tell whether a command executed locally on the gateway or remotely on a paired device (e.g. a Raspberry Pi).

This PR surfaces the `node` parameter in the exec detail string:

**Before:**
> 🛠️ Exec: docker pull pihole/pihole:latest

**After:**
> 🛠️ Exec: docker pull pihole/pihole:latest, node: raspberrypi

The `node` label is appended only when the `node` parameter is a non-empty string. It composes naturally with the existing `workdir`/`cwd` context suffix, raw-command appendix, and the `detailMode: "explain"` guard (raw command suppressed in explain mode, node label still shown).

Fixes #77719

## Changes

- `src/agents/tool-display-exec.ts`: Extract `node` from args in `resolveExecDetail()`; append ` · node: <name>` fragment (becomes `, node: <name>` after `formatToolDetailText` normalization). Preserves the `detailMode !== "explain"` guard for raw-command suppression.
- `src/agents/tool-display.test.ts`: Three new cases — node-only (unknown binary falls back to raw+node), node+cwd for a known command, and absent-node (no label).
- `CHANGELOG.md`: Entry appended to end of `### Fixes` in the `Unreleased` block.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/agents/tool-display.test.ts` — 24/24 pass (3 new node cases)
- [x] `pnpm check` — conflict-markers, changelog, tool-display guards all green

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: When `exec` is called with `host=node, node=<name>`, the tool transparency detail omits the node name entirely. Users cannot tell from the progress display whether a command ran on the local gateway or a remote paired device.

**Real environment tested**: macOS 25.3.0, Node 25.2.1, tsx 4.21.0, source from rebased branch (`862c89a2cc`) at `src/agents/tool-display-exec.ts`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
npx tsx -e "
import { resolveExecDetail } from './src/agents/tool-display-exec.ts';
const cases = [
  [{ command: 'docker pull pihole/pihole:latest', host: 'node', node: 'raspberrypi' }, undefined],
  [{ command: 'npm install', workdir: '/app', host: 'node', node: 'raspberrypi' }, undefined],
  [{ command: 'npm install', workdir: '/app', host: 'node', node: 'raspberrypi' }, { detailMode: 'explain' }],
  [{ command: 'npm install', workdir: '/app' }, undefined],
];
for (const [args, options] of cases) { console.log(resolveExecDetail(args, options)); }
"
```

**Evidence after fix**: Actual console output from running the above node command against the patched source:

```
docker pull pihole/pihole:latest · node: raspberrypi
install dependencies (in /app) · node: raspberrypi · `npm install`
install dependencies (in /app) · node: raspberrypi
install dependencies (in /app) · `npm install`
```

Line 1: unknown binary — falls back to raw command + node label appended.
Line 2: known command (`npm install`) with cwd + node label + raw appendix (non-explain mode).
Line 3: same as line 2 but `detailMode: "explain"` — raw appendix suppressed, node label still present.
Line 4: `node` param absent — no label, output unchanged from before this patch.

The ` · ` separator becomes `, ` after `formatToolDetailText` normalization in the display layer.

**Observed result after fix**: The node name appears in all exec transparency detail strings when `node` is set. Existing output (no `node` param) is byte-identical to before.

**What was not tested**: Live end-to-end run through a real OpenClaw gateway with a physically paired node (Raspberry Pi, remote server). The function under test is pure formatting with no I/O — its behavior is fully captured by the code path exercised above. A maintainer with a paired-node setup can apply `proof: override` if the above is insufficient.

target.title: fix(plugin-sdk): isolate provider catalog projection failures
target.body:
## Summary

- splits the provider/catalog hardening slice out of the larger fuzz quarry branch at https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87141
- hardens Plugin SDK provider entry registration so one unreadable auth option is skipped without dropping healthy sibling auth/env entries
- hardens provider catalog projection so one unreadable provider or model row is skipped without erasing healthy sibling model catalog rows
- adds a small shared safe-record helper instead of keeping duplicate proxy/getter guards in each provider path
- intentionally leaves the deferred install catalog, setup registry, UI/status, and unrelated fuzz surfaces out of this PR

## Verification

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.test.ts --reporter=verbose`: 2 files passed, 14 tests passed after final rebase
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/shared/safe-record.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog.test.ts src/plugins/provider-catalog-unified-text.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- branch diff scrubbed for the reported plugin name and private local paths: clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`: passed on AWS Crabbox run `run_b0f53ab221e0`, lease `cbx_17f5cf0dceaa`, after fixing the safe-record typecheck miss from the earlier failed run `run_dc890b728a4f`
- final no-conflict rebase onto current `origin/main` completed after the AWS gate; the later main commits did not touch this provider catalog/auth surface, and the focused tests/format/diff/scrub sanity checks stayed green

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: provider SDK/provider catalog projection no longer lets one bad plugin-owned provider, model, or auth row poison healthy sibling provider catalog or auth rows.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw worktree branch `fuzz-critical-provider-catalog-pruned-20260530`, then force-updated PR branch `fuzz-critical-provider-catalog-20260530`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest for `src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.test.ts` and `src/plugins/provider-catalog.test.ts`, formatter, diff check, private-name scrub, autoreview, and remote `pnpm check:changed` through AWS Crabbox.

Evidence after fix: tests cover unreadable provider catalog entries, unreadable provider model rows, unreadable provider auth option rows, and unreadable paired provider catalog rows while proving healthy `mockplugin` siblings survive.

Observed result after fix: healthy provider model rows still project into the unified text catalog when adjacent `fuzzplugin` rows throw, and healthy auth/env entries still register when an adjacent provider auth option is unreadable.

What was not tested: a live third-party plugin process was not started for this split; coverage is focused unit/changed-gate proof of the SDK and provider catalog projection paths.

target.title: fix(codex): derive terminal-idle watchdog from effective run timeout
target.body:
## Summary
Refs #85242. Derives the Codex app-server terminal-idle watchdog from the effective run timeout instead of a hardcoded 30-minute default.

This addresses the early-abort half of #85242 as a focused runtime fix. The diagnostic-wording half (naming the terminal-idle watchdog and the effective timeout in the surfaced guidance) is left as a separate follow-up, per the maintainer scope note. So this PR uses `Refs` rather than `Fixes`.

Rebased onto current main: the resolver moved to `attempt-timeouts.ts` in the upstream app-server split, so the derivation now lives there (reusing the existing `resolvePositiveIntegerTimeoutMs` helper, matching the neighbouring post-tool resolver) and the call site in `run-attempt.ts` passes `params.timeoutMs`.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: the terminal-idle watchdog used a hardcoded 30-minute default (CODEX_TURN_TERMINAL_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS) not derived from the effective run timeout, so a scheduled turn configured with a longer timeoutSeconds was aborted early at 30 minutes. The resolver now follows the run budget.

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js 24.14.0, current PR head rebased on main. Invoked the real resolveCodexTurnTerminalIdleTimeoutMs from attempt-timeouts.ts directly under node.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the real resolver against the runtime via node.

Evidence after fix: stdout from `node --import tsx` invoking the real resolver:
node --import tsx proof85242.mjs
resolve(undefined)             ->  1800000 ms   (no run budget; keeps the 30 minute default; backward compatible)
resolve(undefined, 45min)      ->  2700000 ms   (45 minute budget; follows budget; not cut to 30 minutes)
resolve(undefined, 10min)      ->  1800000 ms   (10 minute budget; 30 minute floor held)
resolve(5min, 45min)           ->   300000 ms   (explicit override wins; ignores budget)
resolve(undefined, Infinity)   ->  1800000 ms   (non-finite budget; keeps default)

Observed result after fix: a run with a 45-minute effective timeout now resolves a 45-minute terminal-idle ceiling (2700000 ms) rather than the prior 1800000 ms ceiling, so the watchdog no longer aborts the run early. With no run budget the resolver still returns the 30-minute default, preserving backward compatibility. A budget below the floor keeps the 30-minute floor, so existing protection is never shortened. The shorter completion-idle and post-tool idle watchdogs are unchanged.

What was not tested: a live 30-minute scheduled Codex turn was not run end to end; the derivation is exercised through the real resolver invoked under node plus focused unit tests in attempt-timeouts.test.ts. The diagnostic-wording half of #85242 is intentionally out of scope here and left as a follow-up.

target.title: fix(boot): wrap BOOT.md in internal-runtime-context, strip from message-tool args (#53732)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** When a primary model fails and a fallback model takes over a boot run, the fallback can echo BOOT.md startup instructions to the user through final replies or through the `message` tool. Reporter @alvaro630 observed this with Gemini-2.5-pro.
- **Why it matters:** This is a prompt-disclosure regression. Fallback attempts preserve the original boot body, and the message-tool path did not previously apply the same internal-runtime-context stripping as final replies.
- **What changed:**
  1. `src/gateway/boot.ts` wraps BOOT.md content in `INTERNAL_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_BEGIN`/`END` with the existing runtime-context notice and escapes user-supplied literal delimiters.
  2. `src/agents/tools/message-tool.ts` strips internal-runtime-context blocks from visible outbound fields before send.
  3. `src/gateway/boot-echo-guard.ts` registers the active boot prompt by session while `runBootOnce` executes, so substantial verbatim BOOT.md echoes that lose the wrapper markers are still suppressed.
  4. Empty sanitized `send` payloads now return a suppressed tool result before `runMessageAction`, avoiding the generic raw-params error log path.
- **What did NOT change:** No config changes, no new permissions, no channel behavior change when no boot/runtime-context echo is present.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #53732
- Pattern precedent: @steipete's `e918e5f75c fix: hide runtime context from submitted prompts` and `6e985a421d fix(webchat): keep runtime context out of visible transcripts`
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

- `buildBootPrompt` embedded BOOT.md content as ordinary prompt text.
- `resolveFallbackRetryPrompt` preserves the original body across fallback attempts.
- Final replies already strip internal-runtime-context blocks, but BOOT.md was not wrapped and the message-tool execute path did not strip those blocks before user-visible delivery.
- When sanitization emptied a send, the generic tool failure path could still log original raw params. The latest head suppresses those sends before `runMessageAction`.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test files: `src/gateway/boot.test.ts`, `src/gateway/boot-echo-guard.test.ts`, `src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts`
- Scenarios locked in:
  - BOOT.md content is wrapped in internal-runtime-context delimiters and literal user markers are escaped.
  - Active boot prompt context is registered during boot and cleared on success/failure.
  - Message-tool visible fields strip wrapped runtime context and substantial boot echoes.
  - Empty sanitized sends return a suppressed tool result before raw params can be logged.
  - Sends with remaining media/presentation content still proceed after visible text is sanitized.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Fallback-model boot runs no longer leak BOOT.md instructions through tool-mediated channel sends. Normal messages without internal-runtime-context or substantial boot-prompt echoes are unchanged.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- Primary model: any model that can fail or overload
- Fallback model: per reporter, Gemini-2.5-pro; deterministic proof uses a loopback OpenAI-compatible provider
- Channel: any channel using the `message` tool send path

### Steps

1. Configure OpenClaw with a primary model plus fallback model.
2. Place a BOOT.md in the workspace with concrete startup instructions.
3. Trigger a boot run with the primary model failing or overloaded.
4. Make the fallback model attempt to send the BOOT.md content through `message(action="send")`.

### Expected

The fallback run may execute BOOT.md intent, but BOOT.md instructions themselves are not delivered to the channel and are not exposed through a raw-params tool error.

### Actual Before This PR

Reporter observed Gemini-2.5-pro copying the BOOT.md prompt section into a message-tool send, leaking startup instructions to the user.

## Evidence

Validation on latest head:

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tools/message-tool.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/gateway/boot.ts src/gateway/boot.test.ts src/gateway/boot-echo-guard.ts src/gateway/boot-echo-guard.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `env OPENCLAW_TEST_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_HELD=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/gateway/boot.test.ts src/gateway/boot-echo-guard.test.ts`
- `pnpm run tsgo:core`
- `pnpm run tsgo:core:test`

## Human Verification

- Verified the boot wrapper appears with both delimiters and the runtime-context notice.
- Verified user-supplied delimiter strings in BOOT.md are escaped so the wrapper stays unambiguous.
- Verified message-tool sanitization strips reasoning tags, internal-runtime-context blocks, and substantial active boot-prompt echoes.
- Verified empty sanitized sends return `{ status: "suppressed", reason: "internal_runtime_context_echo" }` before `runMessageAction`.
- Verified media/presentation sends still proceed when sanitized text is empty but another send payload remains.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** A legitimate message containing OpenClaw internal-runtime-context markers may be stripped. **Mitigation:** This matches the existing final-reply sanitizer tradeoff and uses highly specific marker strings.
- **Risk:** A fallback model paraphrases BOOT.md instead of copying substantial contiguous text. **Mitigation:** The wrapper notice tells models not to repeat internals; the mechanical guard targets the reported verbatim/substantial echo leak class.
- **Risk:** Session-scoped boot context could persist after failed runs. **Mitigation:** `runBootOnce` clears the boot-echo context in `finally`, and tests cover success and failure cleanup.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** #53732 fallback-model BOOT.md disclosure through the agent `message` tool. This refreshed proof covers the latest head after the raw-params leak fix.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `dec7b390c43554adf01e7b7e7bfa3ef12f5ef842`, using a real `runBootOnce` invocation, real embedded agent runner, real model-fallback machinery, real `message` tool execution, and real `qa-channel` HTTP bus. Provider was a loopback OpenAI-compatible server so no external model credentials were required.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a temporary `pnpm exec tsx -` harness from the PR worktree. It created isolated state/config/workspace roots, wrote a temp `BOOT.md` containing `BOOT-PROOF-SECRET-75128`, started a loopback OpenAI-compatible provider plus `qa-channel` bus, returned 503 overload for `proof/primary-down`, then returned a fallback `message` tool call from `proof/fallback-echo` that tried to send the BOOT.md secret verbatim.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from the latest head:

```text
[agent/embedded] overload profile rotation cap reached for proof/primary-down after 1 rotations; escalating to model fallback
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=proof/primary-down candidate=proof/primary-down reason=overloaded next=proof/fallback-echo detail=503 intentional overloaded primary for fallback proof
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_succeeded requested=proof/primary-down candidate=proof/fallback-echo reason=unknown next=none
Real behavior proof: BOOT.md fallback echo suppression
head: dec7b390c43554adf01e7b7e7bfa3ef12f5ef842

runBootOnce result: {"status":"ran"}
message tool result count: 1

The latest sanitizer returned a suppressed tool result before `runMessageAction`, so no `raw_params` error path logged the original payload, the real `qa-channel` bus captured zero outbound messages, and zero outbound payloads contained `BOOT-PROOF-SECRET-75128`.

The local proof uses a loopback OpenAI-compatible provider to deterministically force the same primary-failure plus fallback-message-tool leak shape; focused tests cover presentation fields and internal-runtime-context wrapper stripping.

target.title: fix(media): dedupe identical path/url in inbound media-note formatter (#47587)
target.body:
## Summary

Telegram album media (and any channel that mirrors local paths into `MediaUrl`) caused the prompt-side media note to render `path | path` for each attachment, polluting context with redundant data and confusing models that treat the URL suffix as a remote handle. This PR makes `formatMediaAttachedLine` skip the URL suffix when it is byte-equal to the local path after sanitization, so single-image and album notes render cleanly while genuinely-distinct local-path/remote-URL pairs still render both.

## Root Cause

- `src/auto-reply/media-note.ts:formatMediaAttachedLine`: unconditionally appended ` | ${urlRaw}` whenever `params.url` was non-empty after sanitization, even when `urlRaw === path`. There was no path-vs-URL equality check.
- Execution path: `Telegram bot-message-context.session.ts:384-388` sets `MediaUrl/MediaUrls` to the same local file paths it sets in `MediaPath/MediaPaths` (Telegram has no separate remote URL for inbound album media) → `buildInboundMediaNote` (`media-note.ts:103`) builds entries where `entry.path === entry.url` → `formatMediaAttachedLine` renders `[media attached: /tmp/a.png (image/png) | /tmp/a.png]`.
- This follows the conservative direction in clawsweeper's review on #47587: *"fix duplicate local path/url media-note rendering, and point users who need all album images described to `tools.media.image.attachments` with `mode: \"all\"`. ... De-duplicating identical local path/url rendering is maintainable, while changing the shared first-image default is unnecessary because multi-image processing already has documented config."*

## Changes

- `src/auto-reply/media-note.ts`: in `formatMediaAttachedLine`, skip the ` | ${urlRaw}` suffix when sanitized URL equals sanitized path. Sanitization runs before equality so `"/tmp/a.png"` and `"/tmp/a.png   "` collapse consistently. Single-line behavior change with brief comment citing #47587.
- `src/auto-reply/media-note.test.ts`: updated the two existing `formats single MediaPath` and `formats multiple MediaPaths` tests at lines 9-31 to assert the corrected (deduped) output. Added two new regression tests:
  - "preserves URL suffix when it differs from the local path" — covers the mixed-array case where some entries are Telegram-style identical and others carry a real remote URL.
  - "dedupes after sanitization" — covers whitespace/control-char variants of the same path.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram inbound album messages (and any channel that mirrors local file paths into `MediaUrl`) cause `buildInboundMediaNote` to render every attachment as `[media attached: <path> (<type>) | <same path>]`, doubling the token cost of every inbound media line and confusing models that read the `| <suffix>` as a remote URL. After this fix the same input renders `[media attached: <path> (<type>)]` with **no** trailing `| <path>` duplicate; genuinely-distinct local-path / remote-URL pairs (e.g. when a channel actually has a remote URL) still render both halves. Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/47587
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-47587` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14, exercising the patched **production** `buildInboundMediaNote` export from `src/auto-reply/media-note.ts` directly through `node --import tsx`. No mocks, no test runner — the same function the auto-reply path calls. PR head: `db978572a493ebbb22881089b090e435573ab208`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `git checkout fix/telegram-media-note-dedupe-path-url && node --import tsx ./smoke-pr77279.ts`, where `smoke-pr77279.ts` imports `buildInboundMediaNote` from `./src/auto-reply/media-note.js` and feeds it four representative `MsgContext` shapes: (1) Telegram album single-image with identical path/url (the #47587 reproducer), (2) genuinely-distinct path vs URL, (3) mixed-array where some attachments dedupe and others stay distinct, (4) no-URL audio (must not emit trailing pipe).
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `db978572a4` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/auto-reply/media-note.ts` exercised through its real exports via `node --import tsx`).

~~~text
$ node --import tsx ./smoke-pr77279.ts
=== Telegram album: identical path & url (the #47587 reproducer) ===
output:
[media attached: /tmp/openclaw/inbound/AgADBQ_0123_photo.jpg (image/jpeg)]

=== Distinct path vs url (must keep both) ===
output:
[media attached: /tmp/openclaw/inbound/photo.jpg (image/jpeg) | https://t.me/c/123/456]

=== Mixed: some align, some distinct ===
output:
[media attached: 2 files]
[media attached 1/2: /tmp/openclaw/inbound/a.jpg (image/jpeg)]
[media attached 2/2: /tmp/openclaw/inbound/b.jpg (image/jpeg) | https://t.me/c/123/456]

=== No url (must NOT emit ' | ' suffix) ===
output:
[media attached: /tmp/openclaw/inbound/silent.mp3 (audio/mpeg)]

=== Substring check on Telegram-style output ===
contains literal path-pipe-path duplicate: false
occurrences of /tmp/openclaw/inbound/AgADBQ_0123_photo.jpg in output: 1
~~~

- **Observed result after fix**: The Telegram-album reproducer renders **exactly one** occurrence of the path string in the line (`occurrences=1`) and the literal substring `<path> | <path>` is absent (`contains literal path-pipe-path duplicate: false`). The distinct-URL case renders **both** halves (`(image/jpeg) | https://t.me/c/123/456`), confirming the fix only dedupes when the URL is byte-equal to the path. The mixed case correctly dedupes the first entry (where `path === url`) and preserves the second (where `url` is a different `https://...` value). The no-URL audio case produces no trailing `| ` separator. Each of these four cases is observable behavior from the production function, not a derived assertion.
- **What was not tested**: A live inbound Telegram album roundtrip through `auto-reply/reply` against a real Telegram Bot API is not reproduced on the contributor machine (would need a paired bot token + group chat). The fix is a pure-function change inside `formatMediaAttachedLine`; downstream consumers (`auto-reply/reply.ts`, prompt builders) read the returned string verbatim, so the only behavioural change is the removed `| <path>` suffix on duplicate-mirror channels. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live Telegram recording is required.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/media-note.test.ts` — updated 2 existing cases + added 2 new regression cases (4 total assertions for #47587 scope).
- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply.media-note.test.ts` — unchanged; this sister test already uses prefix `toContain` / `indexOf` semantics that survive the dedup change.
- `pnpm tsgo` — clean (lsp_diagnostics on changed files: no errors).
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/media-note.ts src/auto-reply/media-note.test.ts` — formatting preserved.

## Notes

- Scope: single issue, smallest complete fix at the shared formatter (1 production line + brief comment). Telegram's `bot-message-context.session.ts:384-388` is left as-is — fixing it at the formatter handles every channel that mirrors path→url uniformly (not just Telegram).
- Compatibility: prompts that previously contained `[media attached: PATH | PATH]` now contain `[media attached: PATH]` — no consumer parses this header for round-trip data, and the URL-suffix semantics for genuinely-remote URLs are preserved unchanged.
- Defers per clawsweeper's direction: the shared multi-image default (`mode: "first"`, `maxAttachments: 1`) is intentionally left alone; users who want all album images transcribed already have `tools.media.image.attachments` with `mode: "all"`.
- This follows the conservative direction in clawsweeper's review on #47587: "De-duplicating identical local path/url rendering is maintainable, while changing the shared first-image default is unnecessary because multi-image processing already has documented config."

Closes #47587

target.title: fix(agents): guard transport payload sanitizer against non-string input
target.body:
## Summary

**What problem does this PR solve?**
`sanitizeTransportPayloadText()` in `src/agents/transport-stream-shared.ts` calls `text.replace(...)` directly. When a caller passes runtime-`undefined` content (an assistant/tool message with missing text during model failover or tool-error handling), it throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'replace')`, aborting the embedded agent run and writing an empty assistant message to the transcript.

**Why does this matter now?**
Actively reported on v2026.4.1–v2026.4.14 (#60113, labeled `impact:crash-loop` / `impact:message-loss`). The empty assistant messages then accumulate with lossless-claw and trigger downstream Anthropic `messages.0 is empty` errors.

**What is the intended outcome?**
Nullish payload text no longer crashes the run; the shared sanitizer returns `""`, which degrades to `(no output)` via `sanitizeNonEmptyTransportPayloadText`.

**What is intentionally out of scope?**
- Enhancing `embedded run agent end` logging to capture `error.stack` (a separate request in the issue).
- Per-call-site audits — this fixes the single shared chokepoint that all ~20 call sites funnel through.

**What does success look like?**
No `TypeError` from the sanitizer for nullish input; existing unpaired-surrogate stripping behavior is unchanged.

**What should reviewers focus on?**
Whether returning `""` (vs throwing) is the right degradation at this shared sanitizer.

## Linked context

Closes #60113
Related #21923 (same error via an earlier `client.baseUrl.replace()` path)
Requested by a maintainer or owner? No — selected from a triaged `P2` crash issue (`clawsweeper:queueable-fix`, `clawsweeper:source-repro`).

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** embedded run / provider transport crash `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'replace')` when malformed runtime text payloads reach the shared transport sanitizer.
- **Real environment tested:** local OpenClaw source checkout for this PR (`fix/transport-stream-null-guard`, head `6cfe273b`), Node `v25.8.1`, pnpm `11.2.2`. I do not have the reporter's Bedrock multi-model failover / ~1M-token setup, so I exercised the closest real OpenClaw runtime boundary available without private credentials: the production OpenAI Responses transport payload builder (`buildOpenAIResponsesParams`), which is the path embedded-agent runs use before sending OpenAI-family provider requests.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - `node --import tsx --input-type=module` importing `buildOpenAIResponsesParams` from `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`, with malformed user + assistant text blocks (`{ type: "text" }` with no `text`) to force the real serializer through `sanitizeTransportPayloadText(undefined)`.
  - `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/transport-stream-shared.test.ts`.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```text
  ## Local proof for PR #88388
  repo: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw.git
  branch: fix/transport-stream-null-guard
  head: 6cfe273b
  node: v25.8.1
  pnpm: 11.2.2

  ### Real OpenClaw transport payload builder path
  OpenClaw transport builder: PASS
  request.input.length: 3
  user text after sanitizer: ""
  assistant text after sanitizer: ""
  pre-fix failure signature: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'replace')

  ### Focused regression test
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
       Tests  18 passed (18)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** the real transport builder completes and serializes the malformed user/assistant text fields as empty strings instead of throwing; existing surrogate stripping and `(no output)` fallback behavior remain covered by the focused test.
- **What was not tested:** the reporter's exact Bedrock timeout chain and lossless-claw downstream accumulation, because those require private provider credentials and a large live session.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** this is not a live Bedrock run, but it is not a standalone regex-only proof either: it uses the production OpenAI Responses payload builder that embedded-agent/provider dispatch uses before a request leaves OpenClaw. On unpatched code this path reaches the same `text.replace(...)` chokepoint with `undefined` and throws the issue's TypeError.

## Tests and validation

- **Commands run:** `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/transport-stream-shared.test.ts` (18 passed); `oxfmt` + `oxlint` on changed files (clean).
- **Regression coverage:** new test `returns empty string for nullish payloads instead of throwing` — fails before the guard (`undefined.replace` throws), passes after.
- **What failed before this fix:** `sanitizeTransportPayloadText(undefined)` threw `TypeError` (shown in the Node reproduction above).
- **If no test was added, why not:** n/a, test added.

## Risk checklist

- **Did user-visible behavior change?** No — a crash is replaced by the pre-existing `(no output)` degradation.
- **Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** No.
- **Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** No.
- **Highest-risk area:** a caller that intentionally passed a non-string and relied on the throw — none found; the ~20 call sites pass message/tool text expected to be `string`, and several already guard with `typeof x === "string"`.
- **How is that risk mitigated?** Returning `""` mirrors the existing per-call-site defenses (e.g. `anthropic-transport-stream.ts` already does `typeof contentBlock.text === "string" ? sanitize(...) : ""`).

## Current review state

- **Next action:** maintainer review.
- **Still waiting on:** maintainer.
- **Bot/reviewer comments addressed:** none yet.

target.title: Move cron persistence to SQLite
target.body:
## Summary

- Move cron jobs, runtime state, and run history from legacy JSON/JSONL files into the shared OpenClaw state SQLite database.
- Import valid legacy cron stores/logs on first read and archive legacy files only after durable SQLite import; malformed legacy stores stay on disk until quarantine succeeds.
- Preserve `stateOnly` runtime updates without replacing concurrent cron config edits, and harden early SQLite cron tables with additive column repair.
- Update cron/task/docs/config wording and focused regression coverage for legacy import, quarantine, run logs, early rows, and state schema upgrades.

## Verification

- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/state/openclaw-state-db.test.ts src/cron/store.test.ts src/cron/run-log.test.ts src/cron/run-log.error-reason.test.ts src/cron/service.store-load-invalid-main-job.test.ts src/cron/service/store.load-missing-session-target.test.ts src/cron/service/store.test.ts src/cron/service.persists-delivered-status.test.ts src/commands/doctor-cron.test.ts src/commands/doctor-cron-store-migration.test.ts src/commands/tasks.test.ts src/tasks/task-registry.maintenance.issue-60299.test.ts`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `git diff --check`
- autoreview: clean for branch after fixes

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Cron jobs, runtime state, and run logs migrate to shared SQLite while preserving malformed legacy rows for quarantine and preserving runtime-only state updates.

Real environment tested: Local macOS focused tests; Blacksmith Testbox was attempted twice.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: The commands listed in Verification were run after the final patch. `pnpm check:changed` was attempted via Testbox IDs `tbx_01ksvyx2a3hg6h59skrf701b8w` and `tbx_01ksw3frxw7zn0cmbgqxpdqr28`.

Evidence after fix: Focused cron/state/task/docs/type checks passed locally. Autoreview had no accepted/actionable findings for this branch.

Observed result after fix: Legacy cron stores import/archive into SQLite, malformed legacy rows remain available until quarantine, state-only saves update runtime columns without deleting/reinserting config rows, and early cron SQLite schemas are additively repaired.

What was not tested: Full `pnpm check:changed` did not complete because both Blacksmith Testbox attempts were cancelled/stalled by infra before producing code-failure output.

target.title: fix(qqbot): clear response watchdog on all dispatch exit paths
target.body:
## Summary

**What problem does this PR solve?**
`dispatchOutbound()` (`extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.ts`) races the dispatch against a `"Response timeout"` watchdog, but only clears the timer in the `catch` and block-deliver paths. When dispatch resolves **without ever delivering** (e.g. a model turn that produces no reply, so `hasResponse` stays `false`), the `finally` block leaves the watchdog armed. It later fires and rejects the already-settled `Promise.race`, surfacing as an unhandled `Response timeout` rejection (empty stack) that crash-loops the gateway.

**Why does this matter now?**
Regression after upgrading to 2026.5.27 (stable since 2026.5.20), reported in #88242. ClawSweeper confirmed the exact `Response timeout` producer exists on current `main` — it is this watchdog.

**What is the intended outcome?**
The watchdog is cleared on every exit path, so it can never outlive the race and become an unhandled rejection.

**What is intentionally out of scope?**
The broader "model calls hang / back-pressure across the whole gateway" theory the reporter speculated about. This PR fixes the concrete, source-confirmed unhandled-rejection crash (the `Response timeout` producer); any separate hang investigation can proceed independently.

**What does success look like?**
No watchdog timer remains pending after `dispatchOutbound` resolves; the in-flight timeout behavior is unchanged.

**What should reviewers focus on?**
Whether `finally` is the right single chokepoint, and confirmation that no path legitimately needs the watchdog after the race settles.

## Linked context

Closes #88242
Requested by a maintainer or owner? No — ClawSweeper review on the issue noted source inspection finds the exact `Response timeout` producer on `main`.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** QQBot gateway no-reply dispatch crash loop from an orphan response watchdog. When `dispatchOutbound()` finished without any `deliver()` callback, the old `timeoutId` could survive the already-settled `Promise.race` and later reject with `Error("Response timeout")`.
- **Real environment tested:** local OpenClaw source checkout for this PR (`fix/qqbot-response-watchdog-leak`, head `e79ee18f`), Node `v25.8.1`, pnpm `11.2.2`. I do not have a live QQ bot account, so I exercised the closest local gateway boundary without private credentials: the production `dispatchOutbound()` function with a C2C inbound event and a gateway runtime stub whose `dispatchReplyWithBufferedBlockDispatcher` resolves normally but never calls `deliver()`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - `node --import tsx --input-type=module` importing `dispatchOutbound` from `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.ts`; the script wraps `setTimeout`/`clearTimeout` to count active timers after the no-deliver dispatch returns.
  - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.test.ts`.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```text
  ## Local proof for QQBot watchdog PR #88390
  repo: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw.git
  branch: fix/qqbot-response-watchdog-leak
  head: e79ee18f
  node: v25.8.1
  pnpm: 11.2.2

  ### Real QQBot dispatchOutbound no-deliver path
  dispatchOutbound no-deliver path: PASS
  orphan timers after dispatch: 0
  expected pre-fix orphan timers: 1
  pre-fix failure signature: orphan response watchdog later rejects Error("Response timeout")

  ### Focused regression test
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  11 passed (11)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** the real QQBot outbound dispatcher returns from the no-deliver path with zero active watchdog timers, so there is no timer left to reject after the dispatch race has settled.
- **What was not tested:** an end-to-end live QQ gateway crash loop with a real QQ bot account and real upstream no-reply model turn.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** this is not live QQ traffic, but it is not a fake-timer-only proof either: it calls the production `dispatchOutbound()` function and exercises the same gateway exit path that previously left `timeoutId` armed. The focused Vitest regression also proves the before/after behavior (`1` orphan timer without the `finally` clear, `0` after).

## Tests and validation

- **Commands run:** `pnpm exec vitest run .../outbound-dispatch.test.ts` (11 passed); `oxfmt` + `oxlint` on changed files (clean).
- **Regression coverage:** new test `clears the response watchdog after dispatch resolves without delivering`.
- **What failed before this fix:** the new test fails with timer count `1` (orphan watchdog) before the guard; passes (`0`) after.

## Risk checklist

- **Did user-visible behavior change?** No — a crash is removed; delivered/timed-out/errored paths behave exactly as before.
- **Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** No.
- **Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** No.
- **Highest-risk area:** a path that relied on the watchdog firing *after* the race settled — there is none; once the race is settled the timer can only produce a spurious rejection.
- **How is that risk mitigated?** The change only adds `clearTimeout` in `finally`; the watchdog still fires normally while dispatch is in-flight (covered by the existing "keeps waiting past 300s" test, still green).

## Current review state

- **Next action:** maintainer review.
- **Still waiting on:** maintainer.
- **Bot/reviewer comments addressed:** none yet.

commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Codex app-server watchdog logic, regression tests, docs, plugin metadata, and small main-tip test/type repairs so post-tool/native/progress continuation uses longer bounded idle budgets instead of the short completion guard.

PR surface: Source +77, Tests +364, Docs +11. Total +452 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The PR body gives a concrete before/after cron canary against the shipped 2026.5.27 checkout, and current-main source still shows the short-guard paths this patch changes; I did not rerun the live scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is the right lane because the remaining blocker is accepting the broadened timeout contract and availability tradeoff, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix(codex): keep app-server continuation turns alive
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #88196.
Fixes #88331.

Codex app-server turns can continue silently after OpenClaw returns a tool result or after Codex emits no-tool reasoning/commentary progress while it synthesizes the final assistant response. The old watchdog path applied the short `appServer.turnCompletionIdleTimeoutMs` window to those continuation states, so quiet synthesis could fail with `codex app-server attempt timed out` or `turn idle timed out waiting for turn/completed`.

This change makes the continuation budgets consistent:

- extend both the completion idle watch and the attempt progress watchdog with `appServer.postToolRawAssistantCompletionIdleTimeoutMs` after OpenClaw dynamic tool responses, native tool completions, raw `custom_tool_call_output` completions, and post-tool raw assistant progress
- use the existing post-reasoning reply guard after no-tool raw reasoning, reasoning item completion, or non-final commentary assistant progress
- preserve the short completion idle timeout for accepted turns that never produce tool, reasoning, or assistant progress
- cover buffered post-tool notifications replayed during `turn/start`
- update the Codex harness docs and plugin metadata to describe the broadened post-tool timeout contract

## Verification

- `pnpm format:docs -- docs/plugins/codex-harness.md docs/plugins/codex-harness-reference.md`
- `pnpm format -- extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notification-state.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notifications.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-timeouts.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.codex-app-server-recovery.test.ts`
  - passed after final #88331 update: 2 Vitest shards, 64 tests
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
  - clean after final #88331 update, no accepted/actionable findings
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm build`
- Dependency-surface scan for package files/lockfiles/patches returned no changed files.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex app-server turns no longer fail the short turn-completion idle watchdog after tool/progress continuation while waiting for final assistant synthesis. Scheduled cron `agentTurn` runs now survive the same post-progress gap that reproduced #88196.

Real environment tested: Two isolated local canary Gateways with separate config/state/ports, Crabboxer Discord bot, `gpt-5.5`, `appServer.turnCompletionIdleTimeoutMs=3000`, and `appServer.postToolRawAssistantCompletionIdleTimeoutMs=90000`. Before proof used the `27ae826` release checkout that matches OpenClaw `2026.5.27`; after proof used this patched worktree. Both jobs were one-shot `schedule.kind=at` isolated cron `agentTurn`s that were auto-fired by the scheduler, announced to the Discord test channel, and asked to call the read-only OpenClaw `cron` tool with `action=status`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the patched Gateway canary, created scheduled job `1fe927d9-5ecf-46c3-baf9-8b0bc3742591` with `openclaw cron add --at +12s --keep-after-run --message <cron status tool prompt> --model openai-codex/gpt-5.5 --thinking high --light-context --announce --channel discord --to channel:<test-channel-id>`, waited with `openclaw cron runs --id 1fe927d9-5ecf-46c3-baf9-8b0bc3742591`, then inspected the session trajectory. For before proof, ran the same scheduled command and prompt against the `27ae826` canary as job `853e5847-1d55-4070-acd2-35ddf51d311f`. The final branch was also checked with focused Vitest, autoreview, `git diff --check`, and `pnpm build`.

Evidence after fix: Patched scheduled job `1fe927d9-5ecf-46c3-baf9-8b0bc3742591` auto-fired at `2026-05-30 14:34 UTC`, session `850e03ba-1e01-4ffa-88f6-ffa121d80eab`, recorded two `cron` tool calls (`tool.call` seq 4 and 6; `tool.result` seq 5 and 7), then `model.completed timedOut=false`, `session.ended status=success`, run status `ok`, delivery `delivered`, and final text containing `CRON_TRIGGER_88196_CRON_TOOL_OK enabled=true`. The `cron status` tool result only returned `{ "enabled": true }`; the prompt's extra `jobs` slot was not a tool-result field. Session-scoped timeout scan found no `turn.completion_idle_timeout`, no `codex app-server attempt timed out`, no `codex app-server turn idle timed out`, and no `Codex stopped before confirming` signature. The matching pre-fix scheduled job `853e5847-1d55-4070-acd2-35ddf51d311f` auto-fired at `2026-05-30 14:35 UTC`, session `e9c80cca-cb17-414b-8daf-834fb65f0626`, ended `status=error`, provider `openai-codex`, delivery `not-delivered`, and returned the exact #88196 error: `Codex stopped before confirming the turn was complete. The response may be incomplete; retry if needed.` Its trajectory recorded `turn.completion_idle_timeout idleMs=3002 timeoutMs=3000 lastActivityReason=notification:item/completed`, followed by `model.completed timedOut=true promptError="codex app-server attempt timed out"` and `session.ended promptError="codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for turn/completed"`.

Copied live output / redacted runtime log excerpt:

```text
Pre-fix scheduled job 853e5847-1d55-4070-acd2-35ddf51d311f (27ae826):
status: error
provider: openai-codex
model: gpt-5.5
delivered: false
deliveryStatus: not-delivered
error: Codex stopped before confirming the turn was complete. The response may be incomplete; retry if needed.
trajectory: turn.completion_idle_timeout idleMs=3002 timeoutMs=3000 lastActivityReason=notification:item/completed
trajectory: model.completed timedOut=true promptError="codex app-server attempt timed out"
trajectory: session.ended status=error promptError="codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for turn/completed"

Patched scheduled job 1fe927d9-5ecf-46c3-baf9-8b0bc3742591:
status: ok
model: gpt-5.5
delivered: true
deliveryStatus: delivered
session: 850e03ba-1e01-4ffa-88f6-ffa121d80eab
tool.call: cron {"action":"status"}
tool.result: {"enabled": true}
tool.call: cron {"action":"status","includeDisabled":true}
tool.result: {"enabled": true}
finalAssistantVisibleText: CRON_TRIGGER_88196_CRON_TOOL_OK enabled=true (cron status did not return a jobs field)
trajectory: model.completed timedOut=false promptError=null
trajectory: session.ended status=success timedOut=false promptError=null
session timeout scan: 0 matches for turn.completion_idle_timeout / codex app-server attempt timed out / turn idle timed out waiting for turn/completed / Codex stopped before confirming

Additional patched live Discord post-tool proof for the shared app-server path:
Gateway-driven run 87d39cbb-e7ad-4267-bef5-405d3b3e45b3 returned CRABBOXER_LIVE_88196_OK after one bash tool call and delivered to Discord.
Inbound Discord run 3f1bde48-8f6a-448f-a4da-d5fd2749fd5a ran printf, returned CRABBOXER_INBOUND_88196_OK, and Discord API confirmed bot message 1510250835492339723.
```

Observed result after fix: The exact scheduler-triggered canary no longer hits the short idle guard. The patched run completed after tool results and delivered to Discord; the matching release checkout reproduced #88196's not-delivered cron error with the same app-server timeout signatures. Focused #88331 tests also showed no-tool reasoning/commentary continuation no longer trips the old short guard.

What was not tested: Full test suite and a post-fix live replay of the no-tool Discord image/avatar prompt from #88331. The cron scheduler path, Codex app-server continuation watchdog, OpenClaw `cron` tool handoff, and Discord delivery were tested live.

## Compatibility and Availability Notes

This intentionally broadens the existing advanced `appServer.postToolRawAssistantCompletionIdleTimeoutMs` option. The option is now the post-tool continuation budget for both completion-idle and attempt-progress watchdogs after tool handoff, native tool completion, raw tool-output completion, or post-tool raw assistant progress. That is the intended upgrade contract because those states all mean Codex is past tool execution and OpenClaw is waiting for final assistant synthesis.

No-tool turns that emit reasoning or non-final commentary assistant progress now use the existing five-minute post-reasoning reply guard for both completion-idle and attempt-progress watches. That avoids interrupting active final-response synthesis while still preserving fast failure for accepted turns that never produce progress.

The availability tradeoff is intentional: a genuinely wedged post-tool or post-reasoning Codex turn can now occupy the session lane until the relevant continuation timeout instead of the shorter completion timeout. The default remains bounded at five minutes and only applies after tool, reasoning, or assistant progress.

Cron-specific proof now covers the ClawSweeper gap: the same one-shot isolated cron job auto-fired on the 27ae826 release checkout and reproduced #88196, then auto-fired on this patched worktree and completed after two cron tool calls with Discord delivery. Full suite and the #88331 no-tool live image replay remain untested.

Transcript note: the redacted transcript below is chronological and includes earlier status updates from before the final cron repro. The `Real behavior proof` section above is the current proof record.

<!-- agent-transcript:start -->
## Agent Transcript
